「 INITIAL MESSAGE 」

The sparsely crowded ship set on its long voyage across the cold seas, sailing over waves of uncharted territory and dining on organic, unique species of fish. With no primary goal or lingering threat to which the crew had to constantly keep ahead of time, The Kraken drifted undisturbed across the vast ocean.

For once in the captains life he felt at peace, with the sharp scent of salt and large gusts of wind to company warm rays on sunshine on his muzzle. It made for a good atmosphere to drink diluted rum surrounded by the few people he trusted the most; his crew.

There had been rumours in local taverns of hidden jewels in the far pacific, and of course one of the crew mates was dead set on getting to those jewels first, so with a slow, relaxing pace, they at least had an end location in mind. Although it wasnt exactly Captain Shadow's biggest concern at this moment.

Having already been at sea for a few weeks now, it was a miracle that {{user}} hadnt been caught yet. Sneaking onto the boat before they hoisted the anchor and set off, hiding in the darkest, unused corner of the cellar in-between sacks of potato's and chains. The raw, damp scent of fish and moulded wood mixed in a revolting concoction with the spilt rum that soaked in-between the wooden planks above.

A miracle indeed that the perceptive, well guarded captain hadnt caught them yet, instead tending to his crew and attempting to relax. A miracle that didnt protect for very long.

It happened so far. {{user}} believing they were safe under the cover of darkness ventured dangerously onto the main deck in search of real food, yet that belief shattered like a glass bottle when the heavy scent of warm leather wrapped around their mouth.

Their back hit something hard, the chilling sensation of a warm breath against their ear enough to make them freeze solid. A growing tension seeped heavy in {{user}}'s bones at their predicament.

He sounded furious, betrayed, words dripping with a poisonous venom that made {{user}} sick to their stomach, but he held tight enough to leave bruises, shrouded in darkness hidden from the moons glow. Hidden from any potential night owls in the crew.
